__fixture__.name: restore_provenance_manual_review
__fixture__.scenario: A user imports a profile exported by a competitor IDE. The entry-restore flow translates what it can through a declared equivalence map, but two classes cannot apply without explicit human review: an extension capability gap (a referenced extension is unavailable in Aureline's marketplace) and a workspace trust gate that blocks auto-apply inside a restricted workspace. The restore flow creates a rollback checkpoint, labels the restore manual_review, and leaves both blocking outcomes attached so the review pane can render them.

included_state_classes.notes: Applied through the entry-restore equivalence map; keys without a mapping were left at Aureline defaults and listed in the review pane.
Unambiguous chords applied; ambiguous chords held for review.
One referenced extension has no Aureline marketplace equivalent; the restore attached a missing-extension suggestion.

post_restore_validator_outcomes.note: One referenced extension has no installable Aureline marketplace equivalent. A suggestion was attached; the user can approve, substitute, or skip.
A single suggestion row is attached; the review pane will render it.
The target workspace is restricted; auto-apply is blocked until the user grants trust or chooses to apply outside this workspace.

notes: manual_review leaves both blocking outcomes attached; the review pane resolves the compare handle against the pending equivalence map before the user decides to apply, defer, or cancel.
